limitations: AI investment intensity scored 3 rather than 4/5 because disclosed capex figures ($3.0B current investment, $5.1B TEI) are for the broader development portfolio including industrial properties, not exclusively AI/data center infrastructure; no dedicated AI-specific dollar amount is isolated

AI narrative concreteness: data center development is the primary AI-adjacent use case discussed; Prologis is a real estate company enabling AI infrastructure rather than deploying AI internally — the concreteness score reflects named initiatives without quantified AI-specific business results

AI risk disclosure includes named risk categories (system failures, AI-driven cyberattacks, business disruption) with some mechanism detail (how disruption hits operations/reputation), supporting score 4, though no quantified risk exposure or named governance body is disclosed
Excerpts do not contain evidence of Prologis deploying AI tools internally in operations; AI references are primarily about data center real estate development and AI as an external risk factor
